node.title: Uber OA - CodeSignal | 2025 Grad | SDE-1 | 15 June 25

article.title: Uber OA - CodeSignal | 2025 Grad | SDE-1 | 15 June 25
article.content:
Recently completed the **Uber CodeSignal Test** for off-campus 2025 batch hiring. There were **3 questions** in total which were expected to be completed in a duration of **60 minutes**. I was surprised by the fact that there was no proctoring what so ever (atleast I was able to give the test without proctoring).

The questions were of **medium to hard** difficulty level with the applied concepts ranging from simple sorting & searching to complex graphs. I would rate 1st question as easy-medium, 2nd question medium and 3rd one hard.

### Problems:

**Problem-1** There are $$N$$ bombs in a row, each bomb, say $$i$$ (0<=i<N) has a detonation time of $$x_i$$ and time to defuse it is $$y_i$$. Find the minimum time to defuse all the bombs if possible, otherwise -1.

The first line of input is the number of bombs $$N$$. The second line consists of $$N$$ space separated values denoting the time to defuse the bombs. The third line consists of $$N$$ space separated values denoting the time for detonation of bombs.

Input:
```
4
2 1 3 1
4 9 8 2
```
Output:
```
7
```

**Problem-2** There is a magical forest with $$N$$ trees, each tree $$i$$ (0<=i<n) has bear $$a_i$$ fruits. You have a basket which has capacity of $$K$$ fruits (atmost K). Once you enter the forest you are teleported to a random tree, say $$i$$. Choose a number $$L$$ such that you have to collect all the fruits from that random index $$i$$ to $$i+L-1$$ (It is however a gurantee that you will landed to a tree such that $$i < N-L$$). Maximum value of $$L$$.

The first line of input is the number of trees and the value of $$K$$. The second line consists of $$N$$ space separated values denoting the number of fruits on each tree.

Input:
```
4 8
3 1 2 4
```
Output:
```
3
```

**Problem-3** A robot whose battery capacity is $$w$$, wants to ascend a vertical 2D wall ($$N$$x$$M$$). It can start from any cell in the bottom row and wants to reach any cell in the top row. Each cell can be `'x'`, meaning the robot can hop on that cell or a `'.'`, meaning the robot can't hop on that node. However there are some conditions it has to follow -

- It can only move in horizontal (along the same row) or up direction.
- The robo currenty at ($$x_1$$, $$y_1$$) can only jump to ($$x_2$$, $$y_2$$), if the euclidean distance between these two cells is less than or equal to $$w$$ (robot battery capacity).
- Due to some sensor constrints it can hop atmost 2 nodes horizontally (along the same row).

The first line of input is the number of rows, number of columns and the battery capacity. The second line is a 2D wall cell graph.

Input:
```
3 4 1
xx.x
.x..
.x.x
.x..
```
Output:
```
2
```

**Disclaimer:** The description of third question might not be very accurate or polished as I could barely recall the question now.


DON'T FORGET TO **UPVOTE** IF YOU FIND IT USEFUL.
